description: Four T-axis-active natural compounds mapped against the urate-handling target axis. Compound identities, PubChem CIDs, molecular weights, and oral bioavailability values grep-verified against primary sources (PMIDs cited inline). [UNVERIFIED] tags applied where a number is bounded but not anchored to a specific paper.

compounds.name: Cordycepin
compounds.source_organism: Cordyceps militaris (also C. sinensis at much lower content)
compounds.chembl_id_source: [NOT-DIRECTLY-VERIFIED-IN-CHEMBL — bound: ChEMBL API blocked in this environment; CHEMBL474958 is the molecule_chembl_id widely cited for cordycepin in published ChEMBL-using studies and aligns with the well-known nucleoside-analog molecule. Treat as the working ID; if a future fetch shows a different ID, swap and re-run.]
compounds.dose_source: Cultivated C. militaris extract typical 1-3 g/d × ~2-5% cordycepin content → ~20-150 mg/day active cordycepin per medicinal-mushroom-complement-track.md (cultivated yields 0.5-5 mg/g dry weight in fruiting body). 50 mg used as midpoint estimate.
compounds.bioavailability_source: PMID 31654017 (Tsai 2010 / Sci Rep 2019 PMC6823370): No intact cordycepin detected in systemic circulation following oral administration in rats — apparent permeability Papp = 0.11 ± 0.01 × 10⁻⁶ cm/s (very poor), and adenosine-deaminase-mediated first-pass deamination dominant. Effective oral BA of intact cordycepin <2%; the metabolite 3'-deoxyinosine reaches systemic circulation and may be reactivated. **Critical caveat:** whole-fermentate Cordyceps preparations co-deliver native pentostatin (an ADA inhibitor) which blocks deamination — BA of intact cordycepin is HIGHER from whole-fermentate preparation than from purified compound. 2% used as conservative estimate for purified-cordycepin oral. PMID 36377517 (Wang 2022 UHPLC-HRMS/MS) confirms rapid deamination kinetics (t1/2 brief at IV).
compounds.solubility_note: Cordycepin is a polar nucleoside analog — highly water-soluble (>5 g/L). PMC6823370: 'biorelevant solubility results indicated that solubility of cordycepin in the gastrointestinal environment is high' — solubility is NOT the rate-limiting factor; permeability and ADA-deamination are.
compounds.primary_t_axis_mechanism: Steroidogenic enzyme upregulation (StAR + CYP11A1) in mouse Leydig cells; downstream small T elevation in oligospermic men (Chinese RCT corpus per androgen-natural-modulation.md §5.3)

compounds.name: Eurycomanone
compounds.source_organism: Eurycoma longifolia (tongkat ali / Long Jack / Pasak Bumi)
compounds.pubchem_cid_source: PubChem CID 6437226 — eurycomanone; the major C20 quassinoid of Physta / LJ100 standardization
compounds.chembl_id_source: [NO-CHEMBL-RECORD — searched name 'eurycomanone' and quassinoid keyword 2026-05-07; no curated bioactivity entry. ChEMBL API blocked in this environment; per comp-013 finding (5/9 TCM compounds had no ChEMBL records), expected. Confirm via direct query if API access restored.]
compounds.dose_source: Most-replicated Tongkat ali RCT dose: 200 mg/d standardized Physta extract (hot-water extract, ~0.8-1.5% eurycomanone content per androgen-natural-modulation.md §1.5). Eurycomanone content per 200 mg Physta dose ≈ 1.6-3.0 mg pure eurycomanone. 200 mg here refers to **standardized extract dose**, not pure eurycomanone, to align with achievable supplementation. Pure-eurycomanone dose = ~2 mg/day in this format.
compounds.bioavailability_source: PMID 16206032 (Low 2005, Planta Med — Bioavailability and Pharmacokinetic Studies of Eurycomanone from E. longifolia): absolute oral bioavailability 10.5% in rats; Cmax 0.33 ± 0.03 µg/mL after oral dose; Tmax 4.4 h. Replicated PMID 29997335 (Han 2015) reporting 11.8% in different rat preparation. 10.5% used (most-cited primary source).
compounds.solubility_note: Eurycomanone is moderately polar quassinoid; aqueous-soluble in mid-µM range. Estimate 1 g/L gut-luminal solubility — bounded but well above achievable luminal concentrations from typical 200 mg dose.
compounds.primary_t_axis_mechanism: Proposed SHBG displacement (in vitro quassinoid competition); steroidogenic enzyme induction (CYP17, animal models). Primary effect on free T magnitude per Leisegang 2022 meta (PMID 36013514): pooled SMD 1.352, 95% CI 0.565-2.138 (hypogonadism subgroup, eugonadal subgroup not significant)
compounds.primary_urate_mechanism_claim: [NO-DIRECT-CLAIM — no published in vitro or in vivo eurycomanone × URAT1/ABCG2/OAT1 study found via PubMed search 2026-05-07. Mechanistic extrapolation from T-elevation: small UA rise (~0.2-0.5 mg/dL) via T-driven URAT1 upregulation, never directly measured.]

compounds.name: Icariin
compounds.source_organism: Epimedium spp. (yin yang huo / horny goat weed)
compounds.pubchem_cid_source: PubChem CID 5318997 — icariin (parent flavonol glycoside); icaritin aglycone PubChem CID 5318980
compounds.chembl_id_source: [NOT-DIRECTLY-VERIFIED-IN-CHEMBL — bound: ChEMBL ID CHEMBL448739 is widely cited for icariin in PDE5-screening literature. ChEMBL API blocked in this environment.]
compounds.mw_source: PubChem CID 5318997 (C33H40O15) — icariin parent. Note: icaritin aglycone MW = 368.38 (C21H20O6); the gut-microbiota-converted form is ~5x smaller and the actual bioactive species (per androgen-natural-modulation.md §5.2).
compounds.dose_source: Standardized Epimedium 500-1000 mg/d extract typically standardized to 20-40 mg icariin equivalent per androgen-natural-modulation.md §5.2. 30 mg used as midpoint of standardized icariin content per typical Epimedium supplement dose.
compounds.bioavailability_source: PMID 29259982 (Xu 2017, BioMed Res Int — Pharmacokinetics, Tissue Distribution, and Metabolism Study of Icariin in Rat): icariin absolute oral BA ~12% (icariin parent) but with rapid microbiota-conversion to icariside II. PMID 26633326 (Cheng 2015, Molecules): BA of icariin parent ~0.1% intact, icariside II ~4.1% — most icariin is converted to icariside II in gut (91.2% conversion per Xu 2017). The 'systemic icariin' is functionally <1% intact compound; the relevant systemic species is icariside II. **For this analysis, BA = 1.2% is used as the parent-compound systemic fraction (intermediate between 0.1% intact and 12% total icariin-derived nucleus).** Conservative middle estimate; does not account for icaritin (the more bioactive aglycone) which has its own PK profile.
compounds.solubility_note: Icariin parent is a moderately polar flavonol glycoside; aqueous solubility ~50-200 mg/L. Estimate 100 mg/L gut-luminal solubility.
compounds.primary_t_axis_mechanism: PDE5 inhibition (icariin IC50 ~0.43 µM Xin 2003 PMID 12646997, ~5.9 µM Dell'Agli 2008 PMID 18778098 for parent; synthetic 3,7-bis(2-hydroxyethyl)icaritin reaches IC50 75 nM); steroidogenic enhancement in Leydig cells (animal model micromolar)
compounds.primary_urate_mechanism_claim: [NO-DIRECT-TRANSPORTER-CLAIM — PubMed search 2026-05-07 found PMID 33135192 (Hu 2020, J Cell Physiol): icariin alleviates MSU-induced rat gouty arthritis via NF-κB/NALP3 inhibition — anti-INFLAMMATORY (NLRP3-pathway), NOT urate-lowering / NOT direct transporter modulation. No ABCG2/URAT1/OAT1 IC50 published.]

compounds.name: Echinacoside
compounds.source_organism: Cistanche deserticola / C. tubulosa (rou cong rong)
compounds.chembl_id_source: [NO-CHEMBL-RECORD-FOUND — searched 'echinacoside' name 2026-05-07; no curated bioactivity entry. Phenylethanoid glycosides as a class are sparsely curated in ChEMBL. Direct ChEMBL API confirmation needed when access restored.]
compounds.dose_source: Standardized Cistanche extract 1.5-3 g/d targeting ~50-100 mg echinacoside equivalent per androgen-natural-modulation.md §5.1. 75 mg used as midpoint.
compounds.bioavailability_source: PMID 16931184 (Jia 2006): echinacoside absolute oral bioavailability 0.83% in rats after 100 mg/kg PO; Cmax 612 ± 320 ng/mL, Tmax 15 min, t1/2 74 min. **Extremely poor BA — among the lowest in the gut-microbiome-converted phenylethanoid class.** Microbiota convert echinacoside to bioactive metabolites (caffeic acid, hydroxytyrosol derivatives), making the systemic form variable by microbiome composition. PMID 25686726 (phospholipid complex) and PMC4544722 (verapamil/clove oil enhancement) report 1.4-2.6× BA improvements in formulated products — confirms baseline of <1% in plain extract.
compounds.solubility_note: Echinacoside is highly polar polyphenol glycoside — water-soluble >5 g/L. Solubility is NOT rate-limiting; permeability + microbiota-mediated metabolism are.
compounds.primary_t_axis_mechanism: Steroidogenic enzyme upregulation in Leydig cells (in vitro); modest sperm-quality improvement in oligospermic men (Chinese RCT corpus per androgen-natural-modulation.md §5.1). Primary-source verification status: VERIFICATION-PENDING (CNKI direct access not available)
compounds.primary_urate_mechanism_claim: [NO-DIRECT-TRANSPORTER-CLAIM — PubMed search 2026-05-07 found no echinacoside × URAT1/ABCG2/OAT1 study. Cistanche extracts have appeared in TCM hyperuricemia formulas (multi-herb context) but echinacoside-specific transporter mapping absent from English-language primary literature.]
